DISTINCT CHARACTER
The Authority Framework
Resource and Research Companion

A curated support guide connecting the intellectual foundations of The Authority Framework to credible experts, texts, and research. Each resource is mapped directly to a specific section of the protocol. Use selectively, not exhaustively.
This is not required reading. It is calibrated support for deeper integration.

The Authority Framework Series | Phase 4: Relational Command | Distinct Character Self-Mastery Series Copyright 2025 Distinct Character | The Sovereign Bureau · A. Solenne Institute · A division of Granite Field Holdings Ltd. Co.
HOW TO USE THIS GUIDE
Navigation Logic

Each resource entry includes four fields: the core idea, why it matters to this protocol, which section it directly supports, and when to engage with it. Timing recommendations are calibrated for low-capacity use. You do not need to consume all of this. Use what is clinically relevant to where you are in the workbook right now.
SECTION I: BIOLOGY OF COMMUNICATION
Core Research Domain: Polyvagal Theory and Somatic Regulation
The Sovereign Bureau · A. Solenne Institute · A division of Granite Field Holdings Ltd. Co. 1
DISTINCT CHARACTER | AUTHORITY FRAMEWORK SERIES RESOURCE AND RESEARCH COMPANION
RESEARCHER Stephen Porges, PhD
Core Idea Founder of Polyvagal Theory. His research explains how the autonomic nervous system shapes social behavior, threat response, and the capacity for safety-based connection.
Why It Matters The foundational science behind the protocol's 80% neurology principle. Understanding Porges makes the Ventral Vagal versus Sympathetic distinction concrete and credible.
Supports Section I: Foundational Pre-Requisite
When to Engage Before
BOOK The Polyvagal Theory in Therapy | Deb Dana, LCSW (2018)
Core Idea A clinical translation of Polyvagal Theory into accessible, practical application. Introduces the concept of the 'autonomic ladder' as a visual map of nervous system states.
Why It Matters Directly supports the Ventral Vagal versus Sympathetic distinction. The ladder model gives users a concrete framework for locating their current state before a conversation.
Supports Section I
When to Engage Before or During

first_nonempty_paragraphs: DISTINCT CHARACTER | AUTHORITY FRAMEWORK SERIES INTEGRATION EXERCISES
DISTINCT CHARACTER
The Authority Framework
Integration Exercises

Protocol-specific exercises for deepening embodied use of The Authority Framework. Each exercise is directly mapped to a section of the workbook. Low-capacity and chronic-illness-adapted options are included throughout.
These exercises are not supplementary. They are the practice layer of this protocol.

The Authority Framework Series | Phase 4: Relational Command | Distinct Character Self-Mastery Series Copyright 2025 Distinct Character | The Sovereign Bureau · A. Solenne Institute · A division of Granite Field Holdings Ltd. Co.
HOW TO USE THESE EXERCISES

Each exercise set follows a consistent structure: a brief orientation, the exercise itself, a tracking prompt, and a low-capacity adaptation. The low-capacity adaptations are not a lesser version. For anyone managing chronic illness, energy-limiting conditions, or high cognitive load, the adapted version is the correct version. There is no hierarchy between full and adapted practice.
CAPACITY RULE: On any day you rate your functional capacity at 4 out of 10 or below, use ONLY the low-capacity adaptation. Do not push through. Forced integration is not integration.
SECTION I: BIOLOGY OF COMMUNICATION
Somatic State Mapping
Exercise 1.1: The Pre-Conversation Body Scan
Before any significant conversation today, pause for 60 to 90 seconds. Bring attention to the body from feet to head. Notice without changing anything.
■ Where is there tension or contraction? Name the location specifically.
■ What is the quality of your breath right now: shallow, held, or full?
■ What is your jaw doing? Your shoulders?
The Sovereign Bureau · A. Solenne Institute · A division of Granite Field Holdings Ltd. Co. 1
DISTINCT CHARACTER | AUTHORITY FRAMEWORK SERIES INTEGRATION EXERCISES
■ On a scale of 1 to 10, how regulated does your nervous system feel right now?
■ What does this number tell you about your readiness to communicate?
Tracking Prompt
After the conversation: Did your regulation number shift? In which direction, and at what point in the interaction? Low-Capacity Adaptation
Three breaths only. Inhale for four counts, hold for two, exhale for six. Then name one physical sensation present in the body. That is the complete exercise.

DISTINCT CHARACTER
The Authority Framework
Quick Reference Guide

A fast-access support sheet for The Authority Framework. Designed for fatigue, cognitive overload, limited bandwidth, or any moment when you need the core of the protocol without re-reading the workbook.
Print this. Keep it accessible. This is the operational layer of the protocol.

The Authority Framework Series | Phase 4: Relational Command | Distinct Character Self-Mastery Series Copyright 2025 Distinct Character | The Sovereign Bureau · A. Solenne Institute · A division of Granite Field Holdings Ltd. Co.
CORE ARCHITECTURE: ONE-PAGE OVERVIEW
The Authority Framework: Five-Layer Structure
KEY DISTINCTIONS
The Sovereign Bureau · A. Solenne Institute · A division of Granite Field Holdings Ltd. Co. 1
DISTINCT CHARACTER | AUTHORITY FRAMEWORK SERIES QUICK REFERENCE GUIDE
Critical Conceptual Divides
Soft Power vs. Hard Power

Soft power is a Ventral Vagal state: regulated, connected, magnetic. Hard power is a Sympathetic state: reactive, demanding, compliance-producing but trust-eroding. The distinction is biological, not stylistic.
Persona vs. Mask

A persona is automatic. It deploys when you are triggered, without your consent. A mask is chosen. It is a strategic tool you control and can remove. If you cannot turn it off when you are safe, it is a persona, not a mask.
Reaction vs. Response
A reaction is immediate and driven by nervous system activation. A response is chosen. The pause is where the choice lives. The pause is not weakness; it is the location of authority.
Potent Language vs. Harsh Language
Potent language is direct, complete, and stripped of hedges. It is not aggressive, cold, or unkind. The goal is clarity, not impact. Do not confuse authority with aggression.
Strategic Masking vs. Wound

Deploying a persona in a hostile environment is survival intelligence. Deploying it automatically because you cannot access your regulated self is a wound. Both may look identical from the outside. The difference is internal: choice versus compulsion.
Boundary vs. Ultimatum

first_nonempty_paragraphs: DISTINCT CHARACTER | AUTHORITY FRAMEWORK SERIES OPTIONAL DEEPENING PATHS
DISTINCT CHARACTER
The Authority Framework
Optional Deepening Paths

Supplementary learning directions for users who want to explore the intellectual terrain of The Authority Framework more extensively. This is not required material. Engage with it only when the protocol itself is fully integrated and you have the capacity for additional depth.
None of this is required. If you are still in active workbook practice, return here later.

The Authority Framework Series | Phase 4: Relational Command | Distinct Character Self-Mastery Series Copyright 2025 Distinct Character | The Sovereign Bureau · A. Solenne Institute · A division of Granite Field Holdings Ltd. Co.
A NOTE ON OPTIONAL EXPLORATION

The Authority Framework is a complete, functional protocol. These deepening paths exist for users who are intellectually curious about the foundational science, who work in clinical or coaching practice and want deeper theoretical grounding, or who have fully integrated the protocol and want to extend their expertise. Do not begin these paths as a way to avoid completing the workbook.
Protocol integrity comes first. If you have not completed Sections I through VI of the workbook, close this document and return to the practice. These paths exist for depth, not detour.
PATH 01
Polyvagal Science: Advanced Study
For users who want to understand the biological substrate of the protocol at a clinical level.
Why This Path Deepens the Protocol

Everything in the Authority Framework rests on Polyvagal Theory. Deeper study here strengthens your ability to locate yourself and others accurately on the autonomic ladder, and to understand why the protocol's sequencing (biology before language before tactics) is not arbitrary.
The Sovereign Bureau · A. Solenne Institute · A division of Granite Field Holdings Ltd. Co. 1
DISTINCT CHARACTER | AUTHORITY FRAMEWORK SERIES OPTIONAL DEEPENING PATHS
Suggested Directions
■ Read: Stephen Porges, The Polyvagal Theory (Norton, 2011). This is the primary source text. ■ Read: Deb Dana, The Polyvagal Theory in Therapy (Norton, 2018). Clinical translation.
■ Explore: Porges's concept of Evolutionary Biology of the Social Engagement System as the neurological basis of soft power.
■ Research domain: Interoception and the role of vagal tone in emotional regulation and social perception.
Practitioner Note: This is dense academic material. Engage in small doses. The goal is conceptual depth, not academic fluency.
PATH 02
Linguistic Power and Sociolinguistics
